title: Disaggregation With Multiple Maps
code: make_rect <- function(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) {   st_polygon(list(matrix(     c(       xmin, ymin,       xmax, ymin,       xmax, ymax,       xmin, ymax,       xmin, ymin     ),     ncol = 2,     byrow = TRUE   ))) }  make_poly <- function(coords) {   st_polygon(list(rbind(coords, coords[1, ]))) }  make_sf <- function(ids, geometries) {   st_sf(     area_id = ids,     geometry = st_sfc(geometries, crs = 3857)   ) }  map_1 <- make_sf(   ids = paste0("S1", 1:4),   geometries = list(     make_rect(0.00, 0.00, 0.45, 0.55),     make_rect(0.45, 0.00, 1.00, 0.55),     make_rect(0.00, 0.55, 0.62, 1.00),     make_rect(0.62, 0.55, 1.00, 1.00)   ) )  map_2 <- make_sf(   ids = paste0("S2", 1:5),   geometries = list(     make_poly(matrix(       c(         0.00, 0.55,         0.62, 0.55,         0.78, 1.00,         0.00, 1.00       ),       ncol = 2,       byrow = TRUE     )),     make_poly(matrix(       c(         0.62, 0.55,         1.00, 0.55,         1.00, 1.00,         0.78, 1.00       ),       ncol = 2,       byrow = TRUE     )),     make_poly(matrix(       c(         0.45, 0.00,         0.45, 0.55,         0.00, 0.55,         0.00, 0.00       ),       ncol = 2,       byrow = TRUE     )),     make_poly(matrix(       c(         0.45, 0.00,         0.78, 0.00,         0.78, 0.24,         1.00, 0.24,         1.00, 0.55,         0.45, 0.55       ),       ncol = 2,       byrow = TRUE     )),     make_poly(matrix(       c(         0.78, 0.00,         1.00, 0.00,         1.00, 0.24,         0.78, 0.24       ),       ncol = 2,       byrow = TRUE     ))   ) )

title: Disaggregation With Multiple Maps
text: aggregation rasters simulated population offsets. second time point higher-density pocket bottom-right polygon introduced second map. Counts simulated fine grid negative-binomial model, summed polygon support.
code: set.seed(1)  template <- rast(   ncol = 8,   nrow = 8,   xmin = 0,   xmax = 1,   ymin = 0,   ymax = 1,   crs = "EPSG:3857" ) xy <- xyFromCell(template, seq_len(ncell(template)))  population_1 <- template values(population_1) <- pmax(1, round(10 + 40 * xy[, 1] + 20 * xy[, 2])) names(population_1) <- "population"  bottom_right_hotspot <- xy[, 1] >= 0.78 & xy[, 2] <= 0.24  population_2 <- template values(population_2) <- pmax(   1,   round(12 + 25 * xy[, 1] + 35 * (1 - xy[, 2]) + 90 * bottom_right_hotspot) ) names(population_2) <- "population"  risk <- template values(risk) <- as.numeric(scale(   sin(2 * pi * xy[, 1]) + cos(2 * pi * xy[, 2]) )) names(risk) <- "risk"  mean_counts_1 <- values(population_1) * exp(-4 + 0.8 * values(risk)) mean_counts_2 <- values(population_2) * exp(-4 + 0.8 * values(risk))  fine_counts_1 <- template values(fine_counts_1) <- rnbinom(ncell(template), size = 8, mu = mean_counts_1) names(fine_counts_1) <- "counts"  fine_counts_2 <- template values(fine_counts_2) <- rnbinom(ncell(template), size = 8, mu = mean_counts_2) names(fine_counts_2) <- "counts"  map_1$response <- as.integer(   extract(fine_counts_1, vect(map_1), fun = sum, na.rm = TRUE)[[2]] ) map_2$response <- as.integer(   extract(fine_counts_2, vect(map_2), fun = sum, na.rm = TRUE)[[2]] ) plot_polygon_counts <- function(x, title) {   ggplot(x) +     geom_sf(aes(fill = response), color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +     scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "counts") +     coord_sf(expand = FALSE) +     labs(title = title) +     theme_void() +     theme(       legend.position = "right",       plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)     ) }  cowplot::plot_grid(   plot_polygon_counts(map_1, "Map 1"),   plot_polygon_counts(map_2, "Map 2"),   nrow = 1 ) plot_raster <- function(x, title, fill = names(x)[1]) {   df <- as.data.frame(x, xy = TRUE, na.rm = FALSE)   names(df)[3] <- "value"    ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = value)) +     geom_raster() +     scale_fill_viridis_c(name = fill) +     coord_equal(expand = FALSE) +     labs(title = title, x = NULL, y = NULL) +     theme_minimal() +     theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) }  cowplot::plot_grid(   plot_raster(population_1, "Time 1 population offset", "population"),   plot_raster(population_2, "Time 2 population offset", "population"),   nrow = 1 )

title: Disaggregation With Multiple Maps
text: package workflow starts combining areal responses, covariate raster, aggregation raster disag_data_mmap object. fit small negative-binomial model AGHQ. example keeps mesh coarse uses one quadrature point vignette remains lightweight.
code: schematic_data <- prepare_data_mmap(   polygon_shapefile_list = list(map_1, map_2),   covariate_rasters_list = list(risk, risk),   aggregation_rasters_list = list(population_1, population_2),   mesh_args = list(max.edge = c(0.5, 1), cutoff = 0.1) )  schematic_data #> Disaggregation data (multi-map) info #> ===================================== #> Time points: 2 #> Total polygons: 9 #> Total pixels: 128 #>  #> Use `summary(...)` for more details. fit <- disag_model_mmap(   schematic_data,   family = "negbinomial",   engine = "AGHQ",   engine.args = list(aghq_k = 1, optimizer = "nlminb") )  summary(fit) #> There are 55 random effects, but max_print = 30, so not computing their summary information. #> Set max_print higher than 55 if you would like to summarize the random effects. #> Summary of disaggregation model (multi-map) fit with AGHQ #> ======================================================= #> Family: negbinomial #> Link function: log #> Spatial field included: Yes #> IID effects included: Yes #> Betas as fixed effects: Yes #> Quadrature Points: 1 #>  #> Parameter estimates: #> ------------------ #> AGHQ on a 5 dimensional posterior with  1 1 1 1 1 quadrature points #>  #> The posterior mode is: -4.037586 0.7223764 -3.85663 -2.477922 -0.2630212  #>  #> The log of the normalizing constant/marginal likelihood is: -29.35156  #>  #> The covariance matrix used for the quadrature is... #>               [,1]          [,2]          [,3]         [,4]          [,5] #> [1,]  0.0206686936 -1.313785e-02  0.0001812960 -0.008217572  2.212679e-03 #> [2,] -0.0131378489  2.545940e-02 -0.0010685351 -0.004978154  3.474388e-05 #> [3,]  0.0001812962 -1.068535e-03  0.9743869041  0.004511493  7.805125e-04 #> [4,] -0.0082175711 -4.978156e-03  0.0045115036  0.869272355 -2.923759e-01 #> [5,]  0.0022126784  3.474685e-05  0.0007804848 -0.292375926  7.153994e-01 #>  #> Here are some moments and quantiles for the transformed parameter: #>  #>                         mean           sd       2.5%     median      97.5% #> intercept         -4.0375860 8.881784e-16 -4.0375860 -4.0375860 -4.0375860 #> risk               0.7223764 2.220446e-16  0.7223764  0.7223764  0.7223764 #> iideffect_log_tau -3.8566301 1.332268e-15 -3.8566301 -3.8566301 -3.8566301 #> log_sigma         -2.4779224 4.440892e-16 -2.4779224 -2.4779224 -2.4779224 #> log_rho           -0.2630212 5.551115e-17 -0.2630212 -0.2630212 -0.2630212

title: Disaggregation With Multiple Maps
text: model prediction contains one fine-scale rate raster map/time point. population offset enters aggregation likelihood, returned rate surface, multiply rate raster matching population raster visualize expected fine-cell counts.  toy example intentionally small, shows main structure: multiple areal maps, population offset raster, optional covariates, model fitting, fine-grid prediction pass multi-map interface.
code: pred <- predict(fit, N = 10) prediction_rasters <- pred$mean_prediction$prediction  expected_counts_1 <- prediction_rasters[["time_1"]] * population_1 expected_counts_2 <- prediction_rasters[["time_2"]] * population_2  cowplot::plot_grid(   plot_raster(expected_counts_1, "Expected counts from map 1", "mean"),   plot_raster(expected_counts_2, "Expected counts from map 2", "mean"),   nrow = 1 )

what: Spatio-Temporal Disaggregation for Maps with Changing Areal Boundaries
title: Spatio-Temporal Disaggregation for Maps with Changing Areal Boundaries
text: DAST fits spatial disaggregation models areal data observed maps polygon boundaries change time. combines polygon responses, optional fine-scale covariates, population rasters infer fine-scale spatial risk surfaces.

title: Spatio-Temporal Disaggregation for Maps with Changing Areal Boundaries
text: can install GitHub : development branch
code: # install.packages("remotes")  remotes::install_github("nripstein/DAST") remotes::install_github("nripstein/DAST", ref = "development")

title: Spatio-Temporal Disaggregation for Maps with Changing Areal Boundaries
text: polygon_shapefile_list: one sf polygon object per time point, area_id response columns default. covariate_rasters_list: optional matching list terra::SpatRaster covariate stacks. aggregation_rasters_list: optional matching list terra::SpatRaster aggregation population rasters; omitted, uniform aggregation weights used. Polygon raster inputs use compatible coordinate reference systems aligned raster grids within time point.

title: Spatio-Temporal Disaggregation for Maps with Changing Areal Boundaries
text: Predictions returned fine-scale rate risk surfaces. aggregation raster represents population exposure, expected fine-cell counts can obtained multiplying predicted surface matching aggregation raster.
code: # polygon_list: list of sf polygon objects, one per time point  # covariate_list: optional list of terra::SpatRaster covariate stacks  # aggregation_list: optional list of terra::SpatRaster aggregation/population rasters  dat <- prepare_data_mmap(     polygon_shapefile_list = polygon_list,     covariate_rasters_list = covariate_list,     aggregation_rasters_list = aggregation_list )  fit <- disag_model_mmap(dat, engine = "AGHQ")  pred <- predict(fit)

title: Spatio-Temporal Disaggregation for Maps with Changing Areal Boundaries
text: straightforward use model-fitting algorithm choice specifying engine argument disag_model_mmap().

title: Spatio-Temporal Disaggregation for Maps with Changing Areal Boundaries
text: Use engine.args pass arguments specific fitting algorithm selected.
code: # AGHQ controls fit_aghq <- disag_model_mmap(     dat,     engine = "AGHQ",     engine.args = list(         aghq_k = 2,         optimizer = "BFGS"     ) )  # TMB controls fit_tmb <- disag_model_mmap(     dat,     engine = "TMB",     engine.args = list(         iterations = 1000,         hess_control_ndeps = 1e-4     ) )  # MCMC controls via tmbstan fit_mcmc <- disag_model_mmap(     dat,     engine = "MCMC",     engine.args = list(         chains = 4,         iter = 2000,         warmup = 1000     ) )  summary(fit_mcmc)

what: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model (via AGHQ, TMB, or MCMC) — disag_model_mmap
title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model (via AGHQ, TMB, or MCMC) — disag_model_mmap
text: Top-level fitting wrapper engine dispatch engine-specific argument handling. Engine-specific controls supplied via engine.args.

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model (via AGHQ, TMB, or MCMC) — disag_model_mmap
code: disag_model_mmap(   data,   priors = NULL,   family = "poisson",   link = "log",   engine = c("AGHQ", "TMB", "MCMC"),   time_varying_betas = FALSE,   fixed_effect_betas = TRUE,   engine.args = NULL,   aghq_k = 2,   field = TRUE,   iid = TRUE,   silent = TRUE,   starting_values = NULL,   optimizer = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model (via AGHQ, TMB, or MCMC) — disag_model_mmap
text: data disag_data_mmap object. priors Optional named list prior overrides. family One "gaussian", "binomial", "poisson", "negbinomial". link One "identity", "logit", "log". engine Character; one "AGHQ", "TMB", "MCMC". MCMC engine uses tmbstan. time_varying_betas Logical; TRUE, time point fixed-effect. fixed_effect_betas Logical; TRUE (default), beta coefficients treated fixed effects AGHQ outer parameter block (current behavior). FALSE engine = "AGHQ", beta coefficients moved TMB random effects integrated inner Laplace step. engine.args Optional named list engine-specific options. Supported AGHQ keys aghq_k, optimizer, outer_derivative_method. Supported TMB keys iterations, hess_control_parscale, hess_control_ndeps, outer_derivative_method. outer_derivative_method may "tmb" (default) "finite_difference". finite-difference option affects outer fixed/hyperparameter optimization Hessian; TMB still handles inner Laplace approximation. Supported MCMC keys chains, iter, warmup, thin, cores, seed, refresh, laplace, lower, upper, control. Additional named MCMC keys passed tmbstan::tmbstan() rstan::sampling(). iter total number Stan iterations, including warmup. aghq_k Deprecated wrapper level; use engine.args = list(aghq_k = ...). Retained backward compatibility. field Logical; include spatial field? iid Logical; include IID polygon effects? silent Logical; pass engine fit function. starting_values Optional named list starting values. optimizer Deprecated wrapper level; use engine.args = list(optimizer = ...). Retained backward compatibility. verbose Logical; print runtime diagnostics. ... Additional arguments. Engine-specific arguments passed via ... deprecated wrapper moved engine.args.

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model (via AGHQ, TMB, or MCMC) — disag_model_mmap
text: fitted model object class disag_model_mmap_tmb,   disag_model_mmap_aghq, disag_model_mmap_mcmc (also   inherit disag_model_mmap).

what: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via TMB + AGHQ — disag_model_mmap_aghq
title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via TMB + AGHQ — disag_model_mmap_aghq
text: Builds TMB ADFun object multi-map disaggregation model, fits model via AGHQ desired number quadrature points.

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via TMB + AGHQ — disag_model_mmap_aghq
code: disag_model_mmap_aghq(   data,   priors = NULL,   family = "poisson",   link = "log",   time_varying_betas = FALSE,   fixed_effect_betas = TRUE,   aghq_k = 1,   field = TRUE,   iid = TRUE,   silent = TRUE,   starting_values = NULL,   optimizer = NULL,   outer_derivative_method = "tmb",   verbose = FALSE )

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via TMB + AGHQ — disag_model_mmap_aghq
text: data 'disag_data_mmap' object ('prepare_data_mmap()'). priors Optional named list prior specifications (see internal helper). family One "gaussian", "binomial", "poisson", "negbinomial". link One "identity", "logit", "log". time_varying_betas Logical; TRUE, time point fixed-effect fixed_effect_betas Logical; TRUE (default), beta coefficients AGHQ outer parameters. FALSE, active betas treated TMB random effects. aghq_k Integer >= 1: number quadrature nodes AGHQ ('1' = Laplace). field Logical: include spatial random field? iid Logical: include polygon-specific IID effects? silent Logical: TRUE, suppress TMB's console output. starting_values Optional named list starting parameter values. optimizer Optional optimizer name passed AGHQ control. outer_derivative_method Character; "tmb" (default) uses TMB's analytic outer gradient "finite_difference" uses finite differences obj$fn outer optimization Hessian construction. finite-difference option supported optimizer = "nlminb". inner Laplace approximation still handled TMB. verbose Logical: TRUE, print total runtime.

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via TMB + AGHQ — disag_model_mmap_aghq
text: object class 'disag_model_mmap_aghq' (list '$aghq_model', '$data', '$model_setup').

what: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via tmbstan MCMC — disag_model_mmap_mcmc
title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via tmbstan MCMC — disag_model_mmap_mcmc
text: Builds shared TMB ADFun object multi-map disaggregation model, samples tmbstan::tmbstan(). engine supports parameter estimation ; prediction implemented MCMC fits.

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via tmbstan MCMC — disag_model_mmap_mcmc
code: disag_model_mmap_mcmc(   data,   priors = NULL,   family = "poisson",   link = "log",   time_varying_betas = FALSE,   fixed_effect_betas = TRUE,   chains = 4L,   iter = 2000L,   warmup = NULL,   thin = 1L,   cores = NULL,   seed = NULL,   refresh = NULL,   laplace = FALSE,   lower = numeric(0),   upper = numeric(0),   control = NULL,   field = TRUE,   iid = TRUE,   silent = TRUE,   starting_values = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via tmbstan MCMC — disag_model_mmap_mcmc
text: data 'disag_data_mmap' object ('prepare_data_mmap()'). priors Optional named list prior specifications. family One 'gaussian', 'binomial', 'poisson', 'negbinomial'. link One 'identity', 'logit', 'log'. time_varying_betas Logical; TRUE, time point fixed-effect. fixed_effect_betas Logical; TRUE (default), active beta coefficients sampled fixed effects. FALSE, active beta coefficients included TMB random-effect block. chains Integer >= 1; number MCMC chains. iter Integer >= 1; total Stan iterations per chain, including warmup. warmup Integer >= 0 less iter; warmup iterations per chain. Defaults floor(iter / 2). thin Integer >= 1; thinning interval. cores Integer >= 1; number cores passed Stan. Defaults getOption("mc.cores", chains). seed Optional positive integer seed. refresh Optional integer >= 0; Stan progress refresh interval. laplace Logical; passed tmbstan::tmbstan(). Defaults FALSE full posterior sampling. lower Numeric lower bounds passed tmbstan::tmbstan(). upper Numeric upper bounds passed tmbstan::tmbstan(). control Optional list passed rstan::sampling(). field Logical: include spatial random field? iid Logical: include polygon-specific IID effects? silent Logical: TRUE, suppress TMB/tmbstan console output. starting_values Optional named list starting parameter values. verbose Logical: TRUE, print total runtime. ... Additional arguments passed tmbstan::tmbstan() rstan::sampling().

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via tmbstan MCMC — disag_model_mmap_mcmc
text: object class 'disag_model_mmap_mcmc' components   stanfit, obj, data, model_setup.

what: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via TMB — disag_model_mmap_tmb
title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via TMB — disag_model_mmap_tmb
text: Builds TMB ADFun object multi-map disaggregation model, fits model maximizing TMB objective approximates uncertainty via optimized Hessian.

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via TMB — disag_model_mmap_tmb
code: disag_model_mmap_tmb(   data,   priors = NULL,   family = "poisson",   link = "log",   time_varying_betas = FALSE,   fixed_effect_betas = TRUE,   iterations = 1000,   field = TRUE,   iid = TRUE,   hess_control_parscale = NULL,   hess_control_ndeps = 1e-04,   outer_derivative_method = "tmb",   silent = TRUE,   starting_values = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via TMB — disag_model_mmap_tmb
text: data 'disag_data_mmap' object ('prepare_data_mmap()'). priors Optional named list prior specifications (see internal helper). family One 'gaussian', 'binomial', 'poisson', 'negbinomial'. link One 'identity', 'logit', 'log'. time_varying_betas Logical; TRUE, time point fixed-effect fixed_effect_betas Logical; TRUE (default), active beta coefficients treated fixed effects. FALSE, active beta coefficients treated random effects inner Laplace step. iterations Integer >= 1: maximum number optimizer iterations. field Logical: include spatial random field? iid Logical: include polygon-specific IID effects? hess_control_parscale Optional numeric vector scaling Hessian steps. hess_control_ndeps Numeric; relative step size Hessian finite-difference (default 1e-4). outer_derivative_method Character; "tmb" (default) uses TMB's analytic outer gradient "finite_difference" uses finite differences obj$fn outer optimization Hessian construction. inner Laplace approximation still handled TMB. silent Logical: TRUE, suppress TMB's console output. starting_values Optional named list starting parameter values. verbose Logical: TRUE, print total runtime.

title: Fit a multi-map disaggregation model via TMB — disag_model_mmap_tmb
text: object class 'disag_model_mmap_tmb' (list '$obj', '$opt',   '$sd_out', '$data', '$model_setup').

what: Detect if a raster layer is categorical — is_categorical_layer

title: Build the TMB ADFun object for multi-map disaggregation — make_model_object_mmap
code: make_model_object_mmap(   data,   priors = NULL,   family = "gaussian",   link = "identity",   time_varying_betas = FALSE,   fixed_effect_betas = TRUE,   field = TRUE,   iid = TRUE,   silent = TRUE,   starting_values = NULL,   optimizer = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Build the TMB ADFun object for multi-map disaggregation — make_model_object_mmap
text: data 'disag_data_mmap' object. priors NULL named list overriding default hyperpriors. family One "gaussian", "binomial", "poisson", "negbinomial". link One "identity", "logit", "log". time_varying_betas Logical; TRUE, time point fixed-effect fixed_effect_betas Logical; FALSE, active beta coefficients included TMB random effects (AGHQ inner-Laplace treatment). field Logical: include spatial field? iid Logical: include IID polygon effects? silent Logical: pass 'MakeADFun()' suppress output. starting_values NULL named list starting values. optimizer Optional; changing arguments used AGHQ. verbose Logical: TRUE, print details throughout including runtime.

title: Build the TMB ADFun object for multi-map disaggregation — make_model_object_mmap

title: Prepare multi-map disaggregation data — prepare_data_mmap
text: Given lists polygon sf's, covariate rasters, aggregation rasters, combines single 'disag_data_mmap' object ready model fitting.

title: Prepare multi-map disaggregation data — prepare_data_mmap
code: prepare_data_mmap(   polygon_shapefile_list,   covariate_rasters_list = NULL,   aggregation_rasters_list = NULL,   id_var = "area_id",   response_var = "response",   categorical_covariate_baselines = NULL,   sample_size_var = NULL,   mesh_args = NULL,   na_action = FALSE,   make_mesh = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Prepare multi-map disaggregation data — prepare_data_mmap
text: polygon_shapefile_list List 'sf' polygon objects, one per time point. covariate_rasters_list Optional list 'SpatRaster' stacks; may NULL. aggregation_rasters_list Optional list 'SpatRaster'; NULL, uses uniform counts. id_var Name polygon ID column 'sf'. response_var Name response column. categorical_covariate_baselines Named list; names categorical raster layers values baseline levels drop (either level labels numeric codes). sample_size_var Name sample-size column (binomial models); may NULL. mesh_args Passed 'build_mesh()'. na_action Logical; TRUE, drop impute NAs instead stopping. make_mesh Logical; TRUE, build spatial mesh polygons. verbose Logical; TRUE, print timing info.

title: Prepare multi-map disaggregation data — prepare_data_mmap
text: object class 'disag_data_mmap' components including   - 'polygon_data', 'covariate_data', 'aggregation_pixels', …   - 'categorical_covariate_baselines' (normalized baseline labels)   - 'categorical_covariate_schema' (internal encoding schema used fit/predict consistency)

what: Process a single time point for prepare_data_mmap() — prepare_time_point
title: Process a single time point for prepare_data_mmap() — prepare_time_point
text: Internal helper called 'prepare_data_mmap()'. time index 't', : 1. Validates polygon sf rasters. 2. Handles NAs response. 3. Builds validates aggregation raster. 4. Extracts merges covariate + aggregation pixel data. 5. Computes coordinates mesh fitting prediction. 6. Computes start/end pixel indices per polygon.

title: Process a single time point for prepare_data_mmap() — prepare_time_point
code: prepare_time_point(   t,   poly_sf,   cov_rasters,   agg_raster,   id_var,   response_var,   sample_size_var,   na_action,   categorical_schema )

title: Process a single time point for prepare_data_mmap() — prepare_time_point
text: t Integer time-point index (used messaging). poly_sf 'sf' polygon object time 't'. cov_rasters 'SpatRaster' covariates time 't', NULL. agg_raster 'SpatRaster' aggregation weights time 't', NULL. id_var Name polygon ID column. response_var Name response column. sample_size_var Name sample-size column, NULL. na_action Logical; TRUE, drop/impute NAs instead erroring. categorical_schema Internal schema 'build_categorical_schema()'.

title: Process a single time point for prepare_data_mmap() — prepare_time_point
text: list elements:   - 'poly_data': data.frame polygon-level info (incl. 'poly_local_id' & 'time').   - 'cov_data': data.frame pixel-level covariates + 'poly_local_id' + 'time' + 'cell'.   - 'agg_pixels': numeric vector aggregation weights per pixel.   - 'coords_fit': coords mesh-building (used pixels).   - 'coords_pred': coords full-extent prediction.   - 'start_end_index': integer matrix 0-indexed start/end polygon.
